title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, was arrested in Miami Beach for allegedly stabbing his friend Brian Gordon during an altercation.
The incident occurred during a bachelor party, where a dispute over women led to Dickerson approaching Gordon in an SUV.
Both men were hospitalized with injuries; Dickerson is charged with aggravated battery and held without bond in Florida.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four 2026 MLS games.
Revolution took an early lead with Ilay Feingold's goal, but St. Louis responded with three unanswered goals.
New England's Leo Campana exited due to injury, with severity unknown; unrelated to his prior injury.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts and New Hampshire residents are being targeted by scammers with fake court notices demanding payment for alleged violations.
The fake notices include misspelled headings and mismatched phone numbers, urging recipients to pay fines via a QR code.
Authorities advise verifying any court-related messages through official channels and to avoid providing personal information.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague during an armed confrontation in her home in 2025.
Fitzsimmons is accused of assault with a dangerous weapon and has pleaded not guilty; her trial begins March 23.
The trial will be a bench trial overseen by Judge Jeffrey Karp at Essex Superior Court, following her jury trial waiver.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Sinisterra Mazariegos, aged 44, hails from Cali, Valle del Cauca, Colombia.
Her marathon run supports Spaulding's mission to aid patients in reclaiming independence and confidence.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at House of Blues with opening act The Chats on March 20, 2026.
The concert featured new songs and classic hits, showing The Hives' lasting popularity with the audience.
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist energized the crowd with his charismatic stage presence and engaging performance.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh from Braintree is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
McHugh, a cancer survivor, began running marathons to honor her uncle, a retired Fire Chief who died from cancer.
After a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh overcame treatments and surgeries to return to marathon running.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and other senators expressed privacy concerns over Meta's proposed facial recognition in smart glasses.
The senators highlighted risks such as real-time surveillance and potential misuse at political events, demanding answers by April 6.
Meta has yet to confirm plans for this technology, as protests, including 'No Kings' rallies, are scheduled in Massachusetts.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals.
The proposal includes new PFAS-free turnout gear due to health risks linked to current equipment, including cancer.
Firefighters express frustration over the delay, citing health risks; councilors seek more time for proposal review.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to reduce costs and focus on native wildlife.
The zoo faces $57 million in necessary repairs while operating costs are nearly $900,000 annually.
Some residents and zoo staff, including veterinarian Bekah Weatherington, oppose the plan, fearing it will lead to zoo closure.
